name: Coquilles St. Jacques
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt a small quantity of butter and stir with buttered bread crumbs. Drain and set aside.
2. In a medium saucepan, add 1/4 cup water, wine, thyme, 1 tablespoon butter, onion, bay leaf, and parsley sprig. Add scallops and simmer for 2-3 minutes. Strain the broth and save, setting aside the scallops.
3. In a small frying pan, sauté the mushrooms in 1 tablespoon butter, lemon juice, salt, and pepper. After 5 minutes, remove the mushrooms and strain the butter mixture into the reserved broth. Set aside the mushrooms.
4. Heat water for a double boiler to simmer.
5. In the double boiler, blend flour and 1 1/2 tablespoons butter together. Add the reserved broth. Slowly add egg yolks that have been beaten with heavy cream. Blend well.
6. Add scallops. Cook until thickened and smooth.
7. Add mushrooms. Pile into oven bowls, top with buttered bread crumbs, and sprinkle with a good dash of Parmesan.
8. Broil until browned.
